responseMessageContents.text: The user is looking at a preview of the IVX26 app and wondering why they're seeing a placeholder Swift app instead of their actual full application. I should check what's currently in the ios directory to give them an accurate explanation.

responseMessageContents.text:
The user is asking for clarification about what they're seeing — they want to confirm that the preview is just the empty native iOS shell, not their actual full app. I should acknowledge that yes, they're correct: what's displayed is the bare Swift template, while their real application with all 254 screens is in the expo folder as a React Native project, with the backend running separately on Render.

The challenge here is that the work.json only registers the iOS and functions apps, so I can't easily switch the preview to show the Expo app instead. I need to offer them a clear path forward: either we register the Expo app so they can preview it, or we start building out the native Swift UI to match their design. I should keep this straightforward and ask which direction they'd prefer.

Correct — what you're seeing in the preview right now is **not** your full app. Here's why:

- You're previewing **IVX26** (the native Swift iOS app), and it's still the empty placeholder — no IVX screens have been built in it yet.
- Your **full app** (254 screens: investing, chat, owner control center, enterprise access, admin suite) lives in the `expo/` folder — the React Native version synced from your GitHub repo. It's also not registered for preview right now, only the empty Swift app and the Cloudflare functions are.
- Your **backend** is live and healthy on Render (`api.ivxholding.com`), serving all 111 API modules.

So nothing is lost — the full product exists, it's just not what this preview window is pointed at.

Two ways forward:

1. **Preview the full app now** — register the `expo/` app so the preview shows your real 254-screen IVX product.
2. **Build native Swift** — start porting the IVX modules into this IVX26 Swift app (the "native end to end" path we discussed; it's a real build effort, starting with owner login + main tabs against your live backend).

Which one do you want? If you just want to see your full app working, option 1 is the fastest.
